dealSummary: is driven by a narrow band of AI startups raising outsized rounds. Tenex. AI, which offers AI-enabled threat detection software, said in March it had raised a $250 million Series B led by Crosspoint Capital Partners.
Upwind, a cloud security startup, announced an up-sized Series B in January, netting $250 million led by Bessemer Venture Partners.

company: For companies so early in their lifecycle, these rounds are unusual but reflect a broader trend of AI-native startups attracting investor attention and capital. dealSummary: For companies so early in their lifecycle, these rounds are unusual but reflect a broader trend of AI-native startups attracting investor attention and capital. Download the report Related research: AI Propels Next Phase of Cybersecurity Investment Side Letters Smart reads that caught our eye. • About 80% of proteins in the human body are too slippery for traditional pills to target. Molecular glue is changing that, and Novartis, Roche, and Lilly have already committed billions to chase it. South Korea's Xcena, an AI infrastructure computing startup, raised a $135 million Series B led by Atinum Investment and IMM Investment at a $570 million valuation.
London-based AI lab Inherent raised a $50 million seed investment led by Index Ventures and Radical Ventures.
Picogrid, a defense tech company building integration software for military systems, secured a $45 million Series A led by Bessemer Venture Partners.
Healthcare data platform developer H1 received a $40 million round led by CVS Health Ventures.
Pittsburgh-based Gray Swan, an AI security and safety startup, raised a $40 million Series A led by Wing Venture Capital and Madrona.
Geordie AI, a London-based developer of a security and governance platform for AI agents, secured a $30 million Series A led by Balderton Capital.
French cybersecurity startup MokN raised a $15 million Series A led by GV.
